blurb:
This little Middle Eastern spot has become my go-to when I want something healthy but filling. It's in a strip mall but don't let that fool you. The family that runs it really knows what they're doing, and the prices are incredibly reasonable for the amount of food you get.

The shawarma is what I usually order, and they pile it high with fresh vegetables and that white sauce that somehow makes everything better. My wife loves the falafel, which is crispy outside and not dry like some places. They also have great vegetarian options if that's your thing.

It's perfect for a quick lunch or when you want to grab something different for dinner without spending a fortune. The portions are generous enough that leftovers are definitely happening.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb: This is the place my wife and I go for anniversaries or when we want to impress out-of-town guests. You're literally dining right on the Chattahoochee River and the views are incredible, especially at sunset. It's definitely a splurge but worth it for special occasions. The Southern seafood is what they do best, and everything feels elevated without being stuffy. Make a reservation and ask for a table by the windows. Just know Sunday dinner ends early at 4pm, then they reopen at 5pm, so plan accordingly.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
This is the place that saves you when nothing else is open. It's been a Marietta institution forever, with that classic neon sign and 24-hour service that actually means something when you need breakfast at 2am or dinner after a late flight. The Greek family that runs it knows how to do both American comfort food and solid Greek dishes.

The breakfast is what most people come for, and those portions are no joke. My husband always gets the Greek omelet and I usually go for pancakes that are bigger than my head. They also do a mean gyro if you want something different, and the Greek salad is actually fresh and worth ordering.

Just know it can get busy during normal meal times, especially weekend mornings. But the staff moves fast and the coffee keeps coming.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb: This is where I go when I'm craving real Indian food and want generous portions that'll feed the whole family. The place is colorful and casual, perfect for a weeknight dinner when you don't want to cook or when you're introducing someone to Indian food for the first time. The biryani is what they're known for and it's absolutely worth it. The rice comes packed with tender meat and the flavors are legit traditional. They serve everything family-style so it's great for sharing, and you'll definitely have leftovers for lunch tomorrow.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
This French bakery is the real deal. The owner is actually French, and you can tell the difference in everything from the croissants to the quiches. It's become my Saturday morning ritual to grab coffee and a pastry here, and the atmosphere feels like you're in a little cafe in Paris instead of South Marietta.

The almond croissants are what I always end up getting, even when I plan to try something else. They also do excellent quiche that makes a perfect light lunch, and those little petits fours are dangerous if you're trying to be good. The baguettes are legit too if you want to take some bread home.

Perfect for weekend brunch, grabbing pastries for a meeting, or when you want to feel fancy without leaving town. Just know Sunday hours are shorter, so don't show up expecting a late afternoon treat.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

blurb: The hype is real here and the atmosphere is always vibrant and fun. Perfect for date night, girls' night, or really any time you want good Mexican food with great drinks. The staff is super helpful if you can't decide what to order. Start with the Esquite because it's packed with flavor and sets the tone. The Pineapple Guava pitcher is refreshing and well balanced, not too sweet or too strong. They're open until midnight most nights which is clutch when you get a late craving for tacos.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This food hall is perfect when your family can't agree on what to eat. There are twenty different vendors under one roof, so everyone can get what they want and still sit together. It's right by the square, so you can walk around after if the weather's nice.

The variety is really good. There's everything from tacos to Asian food to barbecue, plus some local retail shops mixed in. My teenagers love it because they can try different things without committing to a whole meal, and the portions from most vendors are reasonable.

Great for lunch with coworkers, family dinner when everyone wants something different, or if you're showing visitors around Marietta and want to give them options. Parking can be tricky during busy times, but it's worth the hunt.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu & Sun 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb: I was skeptical about the hype but this place really is extraordinary. The thin crust is perfectly crispy and they use ingredients imported from Italy plus local stuff, which you can actually taste. It's become our go-to for family pizza night or when we have friends over. The traditional Italian style means the pizzas are personal-sized, so everyone can order what they want. We usually get a few different ones and share. The salads and antipasti are also really good if you want something lighter to start.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you've never done Brazilian barbecue, this is a great place to try it without the intimidation factor of some of the fancier places. It's all-you-can-eat, so come hungry, and the servers walk around with different cuts of meat on skewers. You flip a little card to green when you want more and red when you need a break.

The picanha is probably the best cut they bring around, and those cheese bread balls are addictive. They also have a solid salad bar and sides like feijoada if you want to try the traditional Brazilian stew. My kids actually love it here because there's enough variety that everyone finds something.

Good for celebrating something or when you want to try a different kind of dinner experience. Just pace yourself because they keep bringing food until you surrender.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu & Sun 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb: This place has earned a spot on my top 5 dinner list in Atlanta and I can't wait to go back. Perfect for girls' night out or when you want to try something different for date night. The vibe is warm and welcoming, and you'll want to try everything on the menu. Start with the hummus and don't skip the wings, which might sound weird for Persian food but trust me. The chicken shish kabobs with the rice with lentils and raisins is incredible, and save room for the tiramisu. Just know they close between lunch and dinner, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
